_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Gig Workers Got SOCSO in 2025; Retirement Floor Still Missing
context: The Gig Workers Bill 2025 brings ~1.2 million platform riders into SOCSO accident and disability coverage for the first time. EPF retirement contributions, sick leave, and broader informal-sector coverage remain outside the framework.

cards.text: Gig workers got SOCSO accident cover in 2025, but still no retirement floor.
cards.sub: The Gig Workers Bill 2025 brings ~1.2 million riders into SOCSO accident protection. EPF retirement coverage and sick leave remain outside the framework.

cards.h: Voluntary opt-in schemes typically struggle to reach the workers most exposed to income shocks.
cards.s: Malaysia's i-Saraan voluntary EPF programme covered 10.7% of self-employed workers in 2022, eight years after launch.
cards.d: Voluntary opt-in schemes typically struggle to reach the workers most exposed to income shocks. Malaysia's i-Saraan voluntary EPF programme covered 10.7% of self-employed workers in 2022, eight years after launch.

cards.h: Platform companies are not legally employers under the Employment Act.
cards.s: The 2022 amendment excluded app-based engagement from the employer definition.
cards.d: Platform companies are not legally employers under the Employment Act. The 2022 amendment excluded app-based engagement from the employer definition.

cards.h: Accident SOCSO is mandatory under the new bill; medical leave and retirement remain unprotected.
cards.s: The first phase covers workplace injury and disability. Income smoothing during illness, and any retirement floor, fall outside the scheme.
cards.d: Accident SOCSO is mandatory under the new bill; medical leave and retirement remain unprotected. The first phase covers workplace injury and disability. Income smoothing during illness, and any retirement floor, fall outside the scheme.

cards.text: Accident coverage closes the cheapest gap. The expensive gaps (retirement, illness, income volatility) remain unaddressed.
Like fitting seatbelts but not building roads. The protection is real, the missing infrastructure is bigger.
cards.sub: Workplace accidents are the easiest risk to insure. Long-tail risks like outliving savings are the hardest, and the framework does not touch them.

cards.text: The Employment Act was written for factories. The 2025 bill is the first real adaptation. It is a floor, not a ceiling.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
